sections.title: Attempting to leave the building before finishing the beginning tutorial

lines.speaker: Gielinor Guide
lines.text: Looks like you're making good progress! The menu you've just opened is one of many. You'll learn about the rest as you progress through the tutorial.

lines.speaker: Gielinor Guide
lines.text: Anyway, I'd say it's time for you to go and meet your first instructor!

sections.title: Talking to the Gielinor Guide again

lines.speaker: Gielinor Guide
lines.text: Welcome back. You have already learnt the first thing needed to succeed in this world: talking to other people!

lines.speaker: Gielinor Guide
lines.text: You will find many inhabitants of this world have useful things to say to you. By [clicking/tapping] on them, you can talk to them.

lines.speaker: Gielinor Guide
lines.text: To continue the tutorial go through that door over there and speak to your first instructor!

lines.speaker: Gielinor Guide
lines.text: Greetings! I see you are a new arrival to the world of Gielinor. My job is to welcome all new visitors. So welcome!

lines.speaker: Gielinor Guide
lines.text: You have already learned the first thing needed to succeed in this world: talking to other people!

lines.speaker: Gielinor Guide
lines.text: You will find many inhabitants of this world have useful things to say to you. By [clicking/tapping] on them you can talk to them.

lines.speaker: Gielinor Guide
lines.text: Now then, let's start by looking at your settings menu.

sections.title: Attempting to chop down a tree before talking to the Survival Expert
Attempting to fish for shrimp before talking to the Survival Expert

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Hello there, newcomer. My name is Brynna. My job is to teach you about the skills you can use to survive in this world.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: The first skill we're going to look at is Fishing. There's some shrimp in this pond here. Let's try and catch some.

sections.title: Attempting to leave through the gate before finishing the survival tutorial

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Hello again. First up, we're going to do some fishing. There's some shrimp in this pond here. Let's try and catch some.

sections.title: After clicking on a fishing spot

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Hello again. You should take a look at that menu before we continue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've managed to catch some shrimp.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Excellent work. Next you'll want to cook those shrimp. To do that, you'll need a fire. This brings us on to the Woodcutting and Firemaking skills.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Next up you need to make a fire. First, you'll need to cut down a tree to get some logs.

sections.title: Attempting to equip an item before learning how to equip items

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Now that you have some shrimp, you're going to want to cook them. To do that, you'll need a fire.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Now that you have some shrimp, you're going to want to cook them. All you need to do is use them on a fire.

sections.title: Speaking to the Survival Expert again

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Hello again. Is there something you'd like to hear more about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about my skills again.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Every skill is listed in the skills menu. Here you can see what your current levels are and how much experience you have.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: As you move your mouse over the various skills the small tooltip will show you the exact amount of experience you have and how much is needed to get to the next level.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: You can also [click/tap] on a skill to open up the relevant skillguide. In the skillguide, you can see all of the unlocks available in that skill.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to hear more about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Woodcutting again.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Woodcutting, eh? Don't worry, newcomer, it's really very easy. Simply equip your axe and [click/tap] on a nearby tree to chop away.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: As you explore the mainland you will discover many different kinds of trees that will require different Woodcutting levels to chop down.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Logs are not only useful for making fires. Many archers use the skill known as Fletching to craft their own bows and arrows from trees.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to hear more about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Firemaking again.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Certainly, newcomer. When you have logs simply use your tinderbox on them. If successful, you will start a fire.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: You can also set fire to logs you find lying on the floor already, and some other things can also be set alight...

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: A tinderbox is always a useful item to keep around!

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to hear more about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Fishing again.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Ah, yes. Fishing! Fishing is undoubtedly one of the more popular hobbies here in Gielinor!

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Whenever you see sparkling waters, you can be sure there's probably some good fishing to be had there!

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Not only are fish absolutely delicious when cooked, they will also heal lost health.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: I would recommend everybody has a go at Fishing at least once in their lives!

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to hear more about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Cooking again.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Yes, the most basic of survival techniques. Most simple foods can be used on a fire to cook them. If you're feeling a bit fancier, you can also use a range to cook the food instead.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Eating cooked food will restore lost health. The harder something is to cook, the more it will heal you.

lines.speaker: Survival Expert
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to hear more about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing thanks.

sections.title: Attempting to chop down a tree
Attemping to pick up an item without sufficient inventory space
Attempting to perform an action outside of the character's range
Attempting to use the range without bread dough
Attempting to leave through the exit before finishing the cooking tutorial

lines.speaker: Master Chef
lines.text: Ah! Welcome, newcomer. I am the Master Chef, Lev. It is here that I will teach you how to cook food truly fit for a king.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already know how to cook. Brynna taught me just now.

lines.speaker: Master Chef
lines.text: Hahahahahaha! You call THAT cooking? Some shrimp on an open log fire? Oh, no, no, no. I am going to teach you the fine art of cooking bread.

sections.title: Talking to the Master Chef before cooking the dough

lines.speaker: Master Chef
lines.text: Time for you to learn the fine art of cooking bread.

sections.title: Talking to the Master Chef again

lines.speaker: Master Chef
lines.text: Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about making dough again.

lines.speaker: Master Chef
lines.text: It's quite simple. Just use a pot of flour on a bucket of water, or vice versa, and you'll make dough. You can fill a bucket with water at any sink.

lines.speaker: Master Chef
lines.text: Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about range cooking again.

lines.speaker: Master Chef
lines.text: The range is the only place you can cook a lot of the more complex foods in Gielinor. To cook on a range, all you need to do is [click/tap] on it. You'll need to make sure you have the required items in your inventory though.

lines.speaker: Master Chef
lines.text: Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing thanks.

sections.title: Opening to go down the ladder before talking to the Quest Guide

lines.speaker: Quest Guide
lines.text: I don't think you're ready to go down there yet.

lines.speaker: Quest Guide
lines.text: Ah. Welcome, adventurer. I'm here to tell you all about quests. Let's start by opening your quest list.

sections.title: Talking to the Quest Guide before opening your quest journal

lines.speaker: Quest Guide
lines.text: Have you not opened that menu yet?

sections.title: Talking to the Quest Guide after viewing the quest journal

lines.speaker: Quest Guide
lines.text: Now that you have the quest list open, you can see all the quests within it. Opening a quest's journal will provide you with more information on it.

lines.speaker: Quest Guide
lines.text: If you haven't started the quest, it will tell you where to begin and what requirements you need. If the quest is in progress, it will remind you what to do next.

lines.speaker: Quest Guide
lines.text: It's very easy to find quest start points. Just look out for the quest icon on your minimap. You should see one marking this house.

lines.speaker: Quest Guide
lines.text: The quests themselves can vary greatly from collecting beads to hunting down dragons. Completing quests will reward you with all sorts of things, such as new areas and better weapons!

lines.speaker: Quest Guide
lines.text: There's not a lot more I can tell you about questing. You have to experience the thrill of it yourself to fully understand. Let me know if you want a recap, otherwise you can move on.

sections.title: Talking to the Quest Guide again

lines.speaker: Quest Guide
lines.text: Would you like to hear about quests again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Quest Guide
lines.text: Within your quest list, you'll unsurprisingly find a list of quests. Opening a quest's journal will provide you with more information on it.

lines.speaker: Quest Guide
lines.text: If you haven't started the quest, it will tell you where to begin and what requirements you need. If the quest is in progress, it will remind you what to do next.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, I'm ready to move on!

lines.speaker: Quest Guide
lines.text: Okay then.

sections.title: Attempting to leave through the exit before finishing the Mining and Smithing tutorial
Attempting to mine without a pickaxe
Attempting to use an anvil before learning how to use it
Attempting to use the furnace before learning how to use it

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Hi there. You must be new around here. So what do I call you? 'Newcomer' seems so impersonal, and if we're going to be working together, I'd rather call you by name.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can call me [player name].

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Ok then, [player name]. My name is Dezzick and I'm a miner by trade. Let's teach you how to mine.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Mining is very simple, all you need is a pickaxe. The rocks around here contain tin and copper. Why don't you get started by mining some?

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: The rocks around here contain tin and copper. You should try mining some.

sections.title: Attempting to mine ore with a full inventory

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Now that you have some ore, you should smelt it into a bronze bar. You can use the furnace over there to do so.

sections.title: Attempting to use an anvil before receiving a hammer
Talking to the Mining Instructor after smelting a bronze bar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a bronze bar. What now?

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Now that you've got a bar, you can smith it into a weapon. To smith something, you need a hammer and an anvil. There's some anvils just here that you can use. See if you can make a bronze dagger.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: See if you can use the anvil here to make a bronze dagger.

sections.title: Attempting to make other bronze items
Talking to the Mining Instructor again

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Would you like me to recap anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about mining again.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Certainly. To mine you need a pickaxe. Different pickaxes let you mine more efficiently.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Earlier I gave you a bronze pickaxe. It's the most inefficient pickaxe available, but it's perfect for a beginner.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: To mine, simply [click/tap] on a rock that contains ore while you have a pickaxe with you, and you will keep mining the rock until you manage to get some ore, or until it is empty.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: The better the pickaxe you use, the faster you will get ore from the rock you're mining.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: You will be able to buy better pickaxes from the Dwarven Mine when you reach the mainland, but they can be expensive.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Also, the better the pickaxe the higher the Mining level required to use it will be. Was there anything else you wanted to hear?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about smelting again.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Smelting is very easy. Simply take the ores required to make a metal to a furnace, then use the ores on the furnace to smelt them into a bar of metal.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Furnaces are expensive to build and maintain, so there are not that many scattered around the world. I suggest when you find one you remember its location for future use.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: An alternative to using a furnace to smelt your ore is to use high-level magic to do it.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: As well as letting you smelt ore anywhere, it has a guaranteed success rate in smelting all ores.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Some metals, such as iron, contain impurities and can be destroyed during the smelting process in a traditional furnace, but magical heat does not destroy them.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Smithing again.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: When you have acquired enough bars of the metal you wish to work with, you are ready to begin smithing.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Take the hammer I gave you, or buy a new one from a general store, and proceed to a nearby anvil.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: By using a metal bar on an anvil you will be presented with a screen showing the objects you are able to smith at your current level.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: It's a pretty straightforward skill as I'm sure you discovered while making me that lovely bronze dagger.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: The higher Smithing level you are, the better quality the metal you can work with. You start off on bronze and work your way up as your smithing skills increase.

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, I'm ready to move on!

lines.speaker: Mining Instructor
lines.text: Okay then.

sections.title: Attempting to climb the exit ladder before finishing the combat tutorial

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: You're not ready to continue yet. You need to know about combat before you go on.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the rat cage without permission

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Oi! Get away from there. Only enter the rat cage when I say so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi! My name's [player name].

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Do I look like I care? To me you're just another newcomer who thinks they're ready to fight.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: I am Vannaka, the greatest swordsman alive, and I'm here to teach you the basics of combat.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Let's get started by teaching you to wield a weapon.

sections.title: Talking to the Combat Instructor before equipping a weapon

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Let's get started by teaching you to wield a weapon.

sections.title: Trying to equip something before opening the worn inventory tab
Talking to the Combat Instructor after equipping a bronze dagger

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Very good, but that little butter knife isn't going to protect you much. Let's get you something a bit better. Once you're properly equipped we can move on to some actual combat.

sections.title: Attempting to unequip an item with a full inventory
Talking to the Combat Instructor before equipping a bronze sword and wooden shield

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Let's get you equipped.

sections.title: After equipping a bronze sword and wooden shield
Talking to the Combat Instructor before opening the combat interface

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: You should check out the combat interface.

sections.title: Talking to the Combat Instructor before killing a giant rat

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Time for some actual combat! Head into the pen through the nearby gate and kill one of those rats.

sections.title: Attempting to attack a 2nd giant rat with melee
Talking to the combat instructor after killing the first giant rat

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did it! I killed a giant rat!

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: I saw. You seem better at this than I thought. Now that you have grasped basic swordplay, let's move on.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Next up we have ranged combat. With this you can kill foes from a distance. With ranged, you'll be able to attack the rats without entering the pit meaning they won't be able to fight back.

sections.title: Talking to the combat instructor before killing the second giant rat

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Use your bow and arrows to kill one of those rats from outside the pen.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the rat cage again after receiving ranging gear

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: No, don't enter the pit. Range the rats from outside the cage.

sections.title: After killing a second giant rat
Talking to the Combat Instructor again

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about combat stats again.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Certainly. You have three main combat stats: Strength, Defence and Attack.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Strength determines the maximum hit you will be able to deal with your blows, Defence determines the amount of damage you will be able to defend and Attack determines the accuracy of your blows.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Other stats are used in combat such as Prayer, Hitpoints, Magic and Ranged. All of these stats can go towards determining your combat level, which is shown near the top of your combat interface screen.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: You will find out on the mainland that certain items can also affect your stats. There are potions that can be drunk that can alter your stats temporarily, such as raising Strength.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: You will also raise your Defence and Attack values by using different weapons and armours.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Before going into combat with an opponent it is wise to put the mouse over them and see what combat level they are.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Before going into combat with an opponent it is wise to see what combat level they are.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Green coloured writing usually means it will be an easy fight for you, red means you will probably lose, yellow means they are around your level and orange means they are slightly stronger.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Sometimes things will go your way, sometimes they won't. There is no such thing as a guaranteed win, but if the odds are on your side, you stand the best chance of walking away victorious.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Now, was there something else you wanted to hear about again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about melee combat again.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Ah, my speciality. Close combat fighting, which is also known as melee fighting, covers all combat done at close range to your opponent.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Melee fighting depends entirely upon your three basic combat stats: Attack, Defence, and Strength.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Also, of course, it depends on the quality of your armour and weaponry. A high-levelled fighter with good armour and weapons will be deadly up close.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: If this is the path you wish to take, remember your success depends on getting as close to your enemy as quickly as possible.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Personally, I consider the fine art of melee combat to be the ONLY combat method worth using.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Now, did you want to hear anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about ranged combat again.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Thinking of being a ranger, eh? Well, okay. I don't enjoy it myself, but I can see the appeal.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Ranging employs a lot of different weapons as a skill, not just the shortbow you have there. Spears, throwing knives, and crossbows are all used best at a distance from your enemy.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Now, those rats were easy pickings, but on the mainland you will be very lucky if you can find a spot where you can shoot at your enemies without them being able to retaliate.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: At close range, rangers often do badly in combat. Your best tactic as a ranger is to hit and run, keeping your foe at a distance.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Your effectiveness as a ranger is almost entirely dependent on your Ranged stat. As with all skills, the more you train it, the more powerful it will become.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Anything else you wanted to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Wilderness again.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Ah yes, the Wilderness. It is a place of evil, mark my words. Many is the colleague I have lost in that foul place.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: It is also a place of both adventure and wealth, so if you are brave enough and strong enough to survive it, you will make a killing. Literally!

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: It is also the only place in the land of Gielinor where players are able to attack each other at will, and as such is the haunt of many Player Killers, or PKers if you will.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: There are a few things different in the Wilderness in comparison to the rest of the lands of Gielinor. Firstly, as I just mentioned, you can and will be attacked by other players.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: For this reason, you will be given a warning when you approach the Wilderness, as it is not a place you would wish to enter by accident.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Secondly, there are a number of 'levels' to it. The further into it you travel, the greater the range of people you can attack.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: In level 1 wilderness you will only be able to attack, or be attacked by, those players within one combat level of yourself.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: In level 50, any player within fifty levels of you will be able to attack, or be attacked by you. Always keep an eye on what level of the Wilderness you are currently in.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Your current Wilderness level is shown at the bottom right of the screen. The final thing you should know about the Wilderness is being 'skulled'.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: If you attack another player without them having attacked first, you will gain a skull above your character's head.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: What this means is that if you die while skulled, you will lose EVERYTHING that your character was carrying.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: When skulled, you should try to avoid dying for the twenty minutes or so it will take for the skull to wear off.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: If you wish to find the Wilderness, head north from where you start on the mainland. It is rather large and hard to miss.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: If you don't wish to end up there, take notice of the warning you will receive when getting near to it.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Now, was there anything more?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, I'm ready to move on!

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Okay then.

sections.title: Attempting to attack a 3rd giant rat with ranged
Attempting to access the Account Guide's room before opening the bank
Attempting to access the Account Guide's room before accessing the poll booth
Attempting to leave through the bank exit

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: I'm the Account Guide. I'm here to tell you about your account.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's an account?

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: Your character IS your account! It's what you are using to play the game right now. Everyone here has one and some people even have multiple!

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: There's various things you can do with your account which is where the Account Management menu comes into play. Let's take a look at it now.

sections.title: Talking to the Account Guide before opening the Account Management menu

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: Before we go any further, you should have a look at the Account Management menu.

sections.title: Talking to the Account Guide after opening the Account Management menu

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: As you can see, there's a few things you can do with your account.

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: First, you have membership. All accounts are free to play by default but if you desire, you can buy membership to unlock members' worlds. These worlds give you access to extra skills, areas, quests, and more!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a world?

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: A world is what you play the game on. Most worlds can hold up to 2000 players. They are split into two main types: free worlds and members' worlds. You can swap worlds with the World Switcher in the Logout menu.

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: You are currently on a free world. If you want to switch to a members’ world and enjoy all of the benefits, you’ll need to purchase membership. You can do so using the store in the Account Management menu.

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: You are currently on a members' world so you already have access to everything that being a member has to offer. Enjoy!

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: Bonds are another thing that you should know about. You can purchase Bonds from the store in the Account Management menu. Once you have some Bonds, you can redeem them for membership!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why would I use a Bond instead of just buying membership normally?

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: Unlike membership, Bonds can be traded with other players. You could purchase a Bond and then sell it to another player for some gold. Alternatively, you could use your gold to buy a Bond from another player!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if I used my gold to buy a Bond from another player, I could get membership without spending any real money?

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: Exactly!

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: Next up, you have your inbox and name changer. The inbox is where you’ll receive important information related to your account and the game. Be sure to keep an eye on it.

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: The name changer allows you to change your display name if you don't like your current one. Be aware though that this is only available to accounts with membership.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Awesome. Is there anything else I should know about accounts?

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: Your account is very valuable and it's important you look after it. Various tools can help with this, such as Bank PINs and two-factor authentication options.

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: You'll find more information on account security on the Customer Support webpage. You can use the Useful Links section of the Account Management menu to get there.

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: Anyway, that's everything I have to tell you about accounts. Let me know if you need a recap, otherwise head on to your next instructor.

sections.title: Talking to the Account Guide again

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: Hello. Would you like to hear about your account again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: Excellent. Well there's a few things you can do with your account.

lines.speaker: Account Guide
lines.text: First, you have membership. All accounts are free to play by default but if you desire, you can buy membership to unlock members' worlds. These worlds give you access to extra skills, areas, quests, and more!

sections.title: Attempting to leave through the chapel exit
Praying at the altar with full prayer points

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, brother.

lines.speaker: Brother Brace
lines.text: Hello, I'm Brother Brace. I'm here to tell you all about Prayer.

sections.title: Talking to Brother Brace before opening a highlighted menu

lines.speaker: Brother Brace
lines.text: You should open the indicated menu to continue.

sections.title: Talking to Brother Brace after opening the prayer menu

lines.speaker: Brother Brace
lines.text: This is your Prayer list. Prayers can help a lot in combat. [Click]/[Tap] on the prayer you wish to use to activate it, and [click]/[tap] it again to deactivate it.

lines.speaker: Brother Brace
lines.text: Active prayers will drain your Prayer Points, which you can recharge by finding an altar or other holy spot and praying there.

lines.speaker: Brother Brace
lines.text: As you noticed, most enemies will drop bones when defeated. Burying bones, by [clicking]/[tapping] them in your inventory, will gain you Prayer experience.

lines.speaker: Brother Brace
lines.text: That's all I have to teach you for now. If you need a reminder about your prayers, just speak to me again.

lines.speaker: Brother Brace
lines.text: Peace be with you, my child. Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Brother Brace
lines.text: Ah, yes, your prayer list. Prayers can help a lot in combat. [Click/Tap] on the prayer you wish to use to activate it, and [click/tap] it again to deactivate it.

lines.speaker: Brother Brace
lines.text: Active prayers will drain your Prayer Points, which you can recharge by finding an altar or other holy spot and praying there.

lines.speaker: Brother Brace
lines.text: As you noticed, most enemies will drop bones when defeated. Burying bones, by [clicking/tapping] them in your inventory, will gain you Prayer experience.

lines.speaker: Brother Brace
lines.text: Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, I'm ready to move on!

lines.speaker: Brother Brace
lines.text: Okay then.

sections.title: Attempting to access The Node on a regular account

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. I'm Paul, the Ironman tutor. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there Paul.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: Hail, [Ironman type]! What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Ironmen.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: When you play as an Ironman, you do everything for yourself. You don't trade with other players, or take their items, or accept their help.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: As an Ironman, you choose to have these restrictions imposed on you, so everyone knows you're doing it properly.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: If you think you have what it takes, you can choose to become a Hardcore Ironman.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: In addition to the standard restrictions, Hardcore Ironmen only have one life. In the event of a dangerous death, your Hardcore Ironmen status will be downgraded to that of a standard Ironman, and your

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: stats will be frozen on the Hardcore Iron man hiscores.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: If you have some friends, you could stand alone... Together, as Group Ironmen. Group Ironmen have the same restrictions as regular Ironmen except you can trade your items with each other!

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: If you are a daring bunch, you could even try Hardcore Group Ironmen. With Hardcore Group Ironmen you have a set number of lives, when they reach zero, you will become regular Group Ironmen.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: If you ever decided to invite experienced players to your group, you would become Unranked Group Ironmen, which are no longer shown on the Group Ironman hiscores.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: For the ultimate challenge, you can choose to become an Ultimate Ironman, a game mode inspired by the player IronNoBank.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: In addition to the standard restrictions, Ultimate Ironmen are blocked from using the bank, and they drop all their items when they die.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: While you're on Tutorial Island, you can switch freely between being a standard Ironman, an Ultimate Ironman, a Hardcore Ironman, a Group Ironman, a Hardcore Group Ironman or a regular character.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: Once you've left this island, you'll be able to find my colleague Adam in Lumbridge, but he'll only let you switch your restrictions downwards, not upwards.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: So he will let Hardcore Ironmen or Ultimate Ironmen downgrade to standard Ironmen, and he'll let all types of Ironman become normal players.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: I must warn you that Ironman mode is not recommended for new players. You can still give it a go if you think you're up for the challenge though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to change my Ironman mode.

lines.speaker: Ironman tutor
lines.text: Make sure you've got your Ironman settings all sorted before you leave Tutorial Island. Some things can't be changed later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm fine, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: Good day, newcomer. My name is Terrova. I'm here to tell you about Magic. Let's start by opening your magic interface.

sections.title: Talking to the Magic Instructor before opening the magic interface

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: All you have to do is open the magic interface by [clicking/tapping] on the flashing icon.

sections.title: Talking to the Magic Instructor after opening the magic interface

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: Currently you can only cast one offensive spell called Wind Strike. Let's try it out on one of those chickens.

sections.title: Talking to the Magic Instructor before casting air strike

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: Sorry, I don't have any more runes just now. But I see you have plenty.

sections.title: Attempting to attack the chicken with a non-magic method
After casting air strike on a chicken
Attempting to cast air strike on a chicken again

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: Well, you're all finished here now. I'll give you a reasonable number of runes when you leave.

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: When you get to the mainland you will find yourself in the town of Lumbridge. If you want some ideas on where to go next, talk to my friend Adventurer Jon. You can't miss him; he's wearing light blue armour with

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: a huge sword. There are also many tutors willing to teach you about the many skills you could learn.

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: Excellent, I think that covers everything. When you're ready to go, use your home teleport spell to teleport to Lumbridge!

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: When you get to the mainland you will find yourself in the town of Lumbridge. If you want some ideas on where to go next, talk to my friend the Lumbridge Guide. You can't miss him; he's holding a big staff with

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: a question mark on the end. He also has a white beard and carries a rucksack full of scrolls. There are also many tutors willing to teach you about the many skills you could learn.

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: Excellent, I think that covers everything. When you're ready to go, use your home teleport spell to teleport to Lumbridge!

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: Were you planning to become an Ironman, by the way?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's an Ironman?

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: If you're interested in that, you should go and ask the Ironman tutor before you leave this island. He's standing outside my house.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'd better go and find him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I do want to become an Ironman.

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: If you're interested in that, you should go and ask the Ironman tutor before you leave this island. He's standing outside my house.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'd better go and find him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not planning to do that.

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: When you get to the mainland you will find yourself in the town of Lumbridge. If you want some ideas on where to go next, talk to my friend the Lumbridge Guide. You can't miss him; he's holding a big staff with

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: a question mark on the end. He also has a white beard and carries a rucksack full of scrolls. There are also many tutors willing to teach you about the many skills you could learn.

lines.speaker: Magic Instructor
lines.text: Excellent, I think that covers everything. When you're ready to go, use your home teleport spell to teleport to Lumbridge!
